summary: Executed backend pytest regression for AutoApply Phase 1 APIs and frontend Playwright validation for route rendering plus core interactions (discover jobs, queue, generate docs, run auto-apply, and kanban status interaction). Backend suite passed fully; frontend core flows are working with one minor UI/chart warning.

issues: Recharts emits repeated console warnings: width(-1)/height(-1) during route transitions, indicating responsive container sizing instability.

action_items: Stabilize dashboard chart container sizing to remove width/height negative warnings on navigation.
Add explicit error handling states for initial page data loads (dashboard/jobs/profile/settings/applications) to prevent silent UI failure on API errors.
critical_code_review_comments: Multiple page-level load functions call APIs without try/catch, so transient backend/network failures can cause uncaught promise errors and broken page state.
ApplicationsPage renders all discovered cards in fixed columns without virtualized/scroll constraints, which can degrade usability as records grow.
seed_data_creation: Uploaded a generated TEST PDF resume via /api/profile/upload-cv and updated preferences/settings values for test validation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public endpoint used from frontend/.env. Backend smoke+workflow coverage is now in /app/backend/tests/test_autoapply_phase1_api.py and passed (10/10). UI flow validated for all required routes and key actions; kanban status interaction triggered success toast.
rca of the issue: Dashboard chart warnings are likely caused by Recharts rendering while container dimensions are temporarily unresolved during route transitions/unmount-mount cycles. Mitigation: guard chart render until container has positive dimensions or ensure stable parent sizing/min-height.
